National Sports Apparel · Ops
AI Claude Bot — Field Guide

Ordering adidas through the Claude bot

How an adidas order gets handed to the portal's built-in Claude bot, how the bot fills the CLICK cart for you, and exactly what changes between drop ship, in-house, and to a decorator.

For: ChaseFor: Jered ·The bot fills the cart and stops. A human always reviews and submits.

01 What the Claude bot actually is

Inside the portal, Claude (Bot) is a team member — same as a CSR you'd assign a task to. When you hand it an adidas purchase order, an always-on worker on the shop Mac mini logs into the Adidas CLICK B2B portal, finds every SKU, adds them to the cart at the right sizes and quantities, sets the delivery location, and enters the customer PO number.

Then it stops before submitting and flips the task to Ready to review & order. Nothing is ever placed without a person looking at the built cart and hitting submit. Think of it as the fastest, most careful CSR you've got doing the tedious cart data-entry — never the final "buy" click.

🤖 Queued→ 🤖 Bot working…→ 🛒 Ready to review & order→ ✅ Bot done

Side tracks you'll occasionally see: ❓ Needs your answer (e.g. a size is on long backorder — it asks how to proceed) and 🚧 Bot blocked (a login/2FA wall it can't clear).

Access today Right now the Assign to Claude controls are switched on for the bot-owner account (Steve). This guide is so you both know exactly what the bot does with an adidas order — and can drive or QA it — as we open the buttons up more widely.

02 Where an adidas order starts

Everything begins on a Sales Order with adidas line items. From the SO you create the purchase order(s), choose how each PO ships, and (when you're ready) hand it to the bot. The same SO also exposes a read-only Order status job — the bot reads live CLICK "My Orders" and emails the rep where every PO stands. It never touches a cart.

03 One choice drives everything: how the PO ships

When you click Create PO on an adidas order, you pick the vendor (adidas blanks), and the form makes you answer the one question the bot cares about most — “Choose how this PO ships.” That single toggle, plus whether you attach a decorator PO, is what tells the bot which delivery address to put on the CLICK cart.

04 The three ways an adidas order ships

Same bot, same cart-filling — the only real difference is the Delivery Location the bot sets in CLICK. Here's each one, and exactly what the bot does on the cart.

🏭 In-House PO · “Spec Order”

In-house — we decorate it

adidas ships the blanks to the NSA warehouse. The warehouse receives and counts them in, and we print/embroider here. This is the default for anything decorated on our floor.

What the bot sets on CLICK Delivery Location stays the default National Sports warehouse address. If a leftover one-time address is showing from a previous order, the bot switches it back to the NSA default.
📦 Drop Ship PO · to the customer

Drop ship — straight to the customer

adidas ships direct to the school / program. The warehouse does not receive or count it. Use this for blanks or finished goods that don't come through our floor.

What the bot sets on CLICK Delivery Location → “Add one-time delivery location”, filled with the program's ship-to address (from the SO's ship-to customer). Attention line = the customer name. No PO boxes.
🎨 Drop Ship PO · to a decorator

To a decorator — outside decoration

The blanks are drop-shipped to an outside decorator (Silver Screen, Olympic Embroidery, etc.) who prints them, then sends them on. You attach a Deco PO (DPO) so the decorator knows which job the blanks belong to.

What the bot sets on CLICK Delivery Location → the decorator's address, with the DPO number on the second line (“Attention 2”, e.g. DPO 4471) so the decorator can match the incoming blanks to their job — never merged into line one.
Rule of thumb If our warehouse should receive and count the goods → In-House. If it should ship past us → Drop Ship, and the only question left is whether it lands at the customer or a decorator (decorator = add the Deco PO).

05 What the bot does once you assign it

When an adidas batch is ready you hit 🤖 Assign to Claude. The bot picks up the queued task and works the CLICK cart in order:

  1. Log in to Adidas CLICK

    Reuses the shop's saved CLICK session. If it hits a CAPTCHA or 2FA it can't clear, it stops and marks the task blocked rather than guessing.

  2. Search every SKU at once & “Add all to cart”

    All article numbers in one search, one add-all click — the fast path. Any SKU missing from results is added from its own page, or noted if truly unavailable.

  3. Open the cart & enter the PO number

    Sets Customer PO # to your PO (e.g. PO 3001 WVC), clearing whatever pre-filled account name was there, and reads it back to confirm.

  4. Set the delivery location

    Default warehouse, one-time customer address, or the decorator + DPO line — exactly per the In-House / Drop Ship choice above.

  5. Check availability, then enter sizes

    Surveys each size, hovers the 📅 restock icon on any out-of-stock size, and applies the 14-day rule: restock within 14 days → order it; beyond that → skip the whole SKU and ask you. Then it types each size under the correct header and reloads to confirm nothing was dropped.

  6. Stop, verify, and hand it back

    Confirms the PO#, address, and every size, screenshots the final cart, and sets the task to Ready to review & order. It never checks out.

The one hard line The bot never places, submits, or checks out an order. It leaves a fully built cart at the review step. A person approves the submit — every time.

06 Quick reference

SituationPO choiceDelivery the bot sets on CLICK
We decorate it here🏭 In-HouseDefault NSA warehouse address
Ships to the customer / program📦 Drop ShipOne-time delivery location = the program's ship-to address
Ships to an outside decorator📦 Drop Ship + Deco PODecorator address, with DPO # on the 2nd (Attention 2) line
“Where's my order?”Actions ▾ · Order statusRead-only — reads CLICK My Orders, emails the rep. No cart.

Bot statuses you'll see on the task, in order: Queued → Bot working… → Ready to review & order → you submit in CLICK → Bot done. If it needs a decision it parks at Needs your answer.

Internal working guide for the portal's Claude bot on adidas orders. Screens captured from the NSA Portal. External portals (NetSuite, Adidas CLICK) are described, not shown.
